title: Step 1
text: When the security operations room receives a report of an armed person inside the healthcare facility, it must rapidly verify the source of the report and the exact location of the armed person.

title: Step 2
text: Collect all available security information regarding the armed person, including gender, age, appearance, clothing description, type of weapon, and the number of injured or hostages if present.

title: Step 3
text: Immediately notify the relevant specialized authorities through the approved emergency channels.

title: Step 4
text: Announce Code Silver through the internal overhead speaker system and repeat the announcement several times, while also sending a signal through wireless communication devices to the security teams assigned for emergency response.

title: Step 5
text: Notify facility leadership immediately and provide continuous updates on incident developments.

title: Step 6
text: Prevent patients, staff, and visitors from approaching or entering the location where the armed person is present.

title: Step 7
text: Communicate with the armed person cautiously and carefully when required, without direct confrontation unless directed by the competent authorities.

title: Step 8
text: Attempt to reach injured persons together with medical staff for safe transfer while maintaining the highest standards of protection and safety.

title: Step 9
text: Secure exit routes for persons present in the incident zone and guide evacuation or protected movement according to the evolving situation.

title: Step 10
text: Coordinate and cooperate with specialized security teams and relevant authorities until the incident is fully concluded.

title: Step 11
text: Prepare the required reports, document the incident, and submit the information according to the approved procedures.

title: Step 12
text: Provide the National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management with all required information related to the incident through the responsible authority in the facility.
